capabilities.name: Salvation.E
capabilities.note: Salvation E-GT (NS[4]) — first callable abstraction; used to prove CALL works in the capability test

capabilities.name: Navana.E
capabilities.note: Navana E-GT (NS[5]) — namespace table writer; tested for E-permission validity

capabilities.name: Mint.E
capabilities.note: Mint E-GT (NS[6]) — GT lifecycle manager; tested for E-permission validity

capabilities.name: Memory.E
capabilities.note: Memory E-GT (NS[7]) — physical allocator; tested for E-permission validity

capabilities.name: LED.LED0
capabilities.note: Abstract LED0 RW GT — self-describing hardware LED capability; used in the LED on/off test sequence

capabilities.name: UART.Device
capabilities.note: Abstract UART RW GT — self-describing hardware serial capability; used in the UART TX/RX test sequence
